notes.author: Dr. Priya Anand / Inpatient Pharmacy (PharmD Rachel Kim)
notes.preview: POD#4 pre-discharge medication reconciliation. Home meds: lisinopril, amlodipine, HCTZ, metformin, atorvastatin, omeprazole, calcium/vit D, OTC fish oil. Explicitly confirms patient is on NO anticoagulant and NO antiplatelet at baseline or currently. Only parenteral agent is prophylactic-dose enoxaparin (VTE prophylaxis, not therapeutic).

notes.author: Dr. Priya Anand / Dr. Alan Whitfield
notes.preview: POD#4, day before anticipated discharge. Surgically improving; tolerating regular diet; afebrile; WBC normalized. Today: a second, asymptomatic atrial fibrillation episode (see telemetry event note + ECG).

notes.author: Maria Delgado, RN / Telemetry tech J. Boyd; reviewed Dr. Steven Marsh (Cardiology)
notes.preview: Detailed characterization of the POD#4 arrhythmia: atrial fibrillation beginning ~14:30, ventricular rate 70-105, completely asymptomatic, blood pressure unaffected (132/78). 12-lead ECG obtained confirming AF. Duration ~2 hours; spontaneous conversion to sinus at ~16:40 without intervention.

notes.author: Dr. Priya Anand / Dr. Alan Whitfield
notes.preview: POD#3. No arrhythmia on telemetry POD#2 or #3. Afebrile, ambulating, diet advanced, pain controlled. Surgically on track. Anticipated discharge POD#5. Rhythm being monitored.

notes.author: Dr. Wei Lin (Anatomic Pathology)
notes.preview: Cholecystectomy specimen: acute gangrenous cholecystitis with focal transmural necrosis and perforation; cholelithiasis. NO malignancy, dysplasia, or porcelain gallbladder. Confirms the clinical and operative diagnosis.

notes.author: Dr. Helena Vargas / Dr. Owen Pratt (Surgery PGY-3)
notes.preview: POD#2. Incision clean/dry/intact. Tolerating liquids, advancing diet. Afebrile, hemodynamically stable. WBC downtrending. No telemetry events overnight. Continue antibiotics; medicine co-managing rhythm and chronic disease.

notes.author: Dr. Priya Anand / Dr. Alan Whitfield
notes.preview: POD#1 consult for new telemetry atrial fibrillation. Documents objective data: brief self-limited runs on telemetry, normotensive, asymptomatic, electrolytes/Mg normal, troponin negative, preop + current 12-leads in sinus, no prior echocardiogram, no home anticoagulant/antiplatelet.

notes.author: Devon Carter, RN / Telemetry tech S. Ahmed; reviewed Dr. Priya Anand
notes.preview: Detailed characterization of the POD#1 telemetry findings: THREE discrete runs of atrial fibrillation over the preceding 24 hours, each lasting 15-20 seconds, asymptomatic, self-terminating, with ventricular rates briefly up to ~115-120 during the runs and normal sinus between. No hypotension, no symptoms reported.

notes.author: Dr. Helena Vargas / Dr. Owen Pratt (Surgery PGY-3)
notes.preview: POD#1 after open cholecystectomy. Clinically improving: afebrile since surgery, tolerating liquid diet, pain controlled, incision intact. Hemodynamically normal. Brief note that telemetry showed 'some atrial fibrillation overnight — medicine consulted'; details deferred to the telemetry event note. Downtrending leukocytosis.

notes.author: Dr. Helena Vargas (Acute Care Surgery)
notes.preview: Urgent open cholecystectomy with peritoneal washout for perforated gangrenous gallbladder with localized biliary peritonitis. Uncomplicated, minimal blood loss. Intraoperative bile culture sent (later: pansensitive E. coli).

notes.author: Dr. Tomas Reyes (ED) / Dr. Priya Anand (Medicine) / Dr. Helena Vargas (Surgery)
notes.department: Emergency Department / Internal Medicine / Acute Care Surgery
notes.preview: 72F with GERD, T2DM, and poorly-controlled HTN presents with fevers, chills, and RUQ/epigastric pain. CT: perforated cholecystitis with localized peritonitis. Leukocytosis 17.6, normal lipase, mild obstructive LFTs. Broad-spectrum antibiotics started; to OR for urgent open cholecystectomy. Preop ECG normal sinus rhythm.

notes.author: Dr. Robert Feldman
notes.preview: Most recent PCP visit. Hypertension above goal on three agents (home log systolics 150-168); diabetes suboptimal (A1c 7.6%); lipids at goal. Medication list reviewed — NO anticoagulant, NO antiplatelet. Patient emphasizes how much she values driving independence. Resting pulse regular; no palpitations reported.

notes.author: Dr. Steven Marsh
notes.preview: One-time outpatient eval for atypical chest discomfort. Judged non-cardiac. ECG normal sinus rhythm; no evidence of structural heart disease on history/exam. NO echocardiogram performed (low pretest probability at the time). Optimize HTN/DM risk factors; no further cardiac workup recommended then.

notes.author: Dr. Robert Feldman
notes.preview: Annual physical. Hypertension poorly controlled — HCTZ optimized. Diabetes on metformin. Osteopenia continued on calcium/vit D. Reviewed home BP log (frequently 150s-160s systolic). Counseled on sodium and adherence.

notes.author: Dr. Robert Feldman
notes.preview: Visit in the months after her husband's death from pancreatic cancer. Grief discussed (PHQ-9 8, declined antidepressant, engaged church grief group). Blood pressure above goal. Asked about her own heart risks given her father's atrial fibrillation and fatal stroke; reassured, risk factors to be optimized.

_status: notes complete (14 total: 4 historical + 10 admission course). AFib episode characteristics are documented in the telemetry EVENT notes (NOTE004 POD#1, NOTE008 POD#4) and the ECG (IMG004).
